design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse side-scrolling stages populated with robotic enemies, switch between six weapons (default handgun plus five special weapons), manage limited Gun Energy gauge, defeat stage bosses, repeat across five stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (Gun Energy management) vs. combat capability; player must decide when to deploy special weapons or conserve energy to revert to handgun. Health depletion risk during traversal and boss fights
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Avenging father's death by infiltrating and dismantling terrorist organization responsible for space colony destruction; progression from colony ruins through enemy hideout to final factory confrontation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Weapon switching mechanic with energy depletion forces strategic weapon selection rather than optimal-weapon dominance. Limited aiming (left/right only, no vertical aim except lying down) constrains combat approach and rewards positioning. Boss encounters demand pattern recognition and energy management simultaneously

difficulty.lives_system: Standard NES lives system with Game Over condition
difficulty.continues_system: Continues adjustable via hidden sound test menu (press B ×33 then Start on title screen)
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage checkpointing; completing a stage progresses player forward; defeat requires stage restart
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden stage select (Game Genie code XTUSKTAV) and continues adjustment allow skilled players to practice specific stages or bosses; standard play forces sequential completion

progression.upgrade_system: Five special weapons acquired throughout stages (shotgun, machine gun, homing gun, laser rifle, grenade launcher) with shared Gun Energy gauge; weapons automatically depleted when energy exhausted, reverting to default handgun. Energy capsules dropped by enemies replenish gauge
progression.unlock_mechanism: Weapons discovered as pickups during stage traversal; no permanent unlocks—weapon access resets between playthroughs. Stage select accessible only via cheat codes
replayability_hooks: Five-stage linear structure resets after final boss defeat, encouraging speedrun optimization
Multiple weapon combinations enable different stage approaches
Gun Energy management creates variable strategies for same enemy encounters
Hidden sound test and stage select reward exploration of menus
Regional sprite differences (US version early build vs. JP/EU armor-covered design) create minor visual variation

world_building_techniques: Opening plot exposition: overpopulation drives Earth emigration to space colonies; father dies in colony #428 destruction; Jay discovers floppy disk with plans and father's message
Stage progression reflects narrative arc: space colony ruins → underground concourse → enemy hideout → flying spaceship → factory
Thematic visual design: each stage environment supports story (colonization infrastructure degrading, then enemy control, final industrial destruction site)
Character motivation: personal vendetta (father's death) drives mission rather than abstract save-the-world premise
Minimal in-game dialogue; primary narrative delivered via instruction manual and opening setup

boss_design.philosophy: Stage-ending gauntlets requiring pattern recognition and resource management. Each boss demands player master weapon switching within energy constraints while learning attack patterns. Bosses represent escalating threat reflecting narrative progression through terrorist organization
notable_bosses: Final boss accessible via Game Genie code (TEXINTIA combined with stage select code XTUSKTAV) by selecting 'ENDING[]'

faction_indicators: Terrorist organization responsible for space colony destruction (antagonist faction)
Space colony research team and scientists (father's faction, destroyed)
Jay McCray lone avenger against organized threat
danger_signals: Robotic enemies and security systems escalate through stages
Colony destruction establishes stakes (mass casualty event)
Enemy hideout and spaceship sequences indicate organized, technologically advanced threat
Factory final stage suggests industrial-scale terrorist infrastructure
environmental_storytelling: Stage 1 ruins of space colony—wreckage and deterioration conveys destruction scale
Underground concourse suggests hidden infrastructure, security networks
Enemy hideout: organized control of physical space, fortifications
Flying spaceship stage: mobile threat, escape scenario
Final factory: production/weapons manufacturing capacity, culmination of terrorist operations
